name: Easy Swiss Steak
recipeIngredient: 1 to 2 pounds bottom round or chuck steak, thin cut, approximately 1/2 inch thick
1 can DelMonte Stewed Tomatoes, Mexican Style
Optional: Approximately 1 lb carrots, sliced and added with other ingredients
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the steak with flour or omit if desired.
- Optional: Slice about 1 lb carrots and add them along with the other ingredients.
2. Cut the steak into serving-sized pieces and remove any visible fat.
3. Dredge the steak pieces in seasoned flour (optional). You can also pound the flour into the beef, but it's not necessary as some recipes have successfully skipped this step.
4. Spray a cast iron skillet with cooking spray and heat to medium-high.
- Brown the steak pieces well in the hot skillet.
5. Add the diced tomatoes and half of the canned beef bouillon to the skillet.
- Cover tightly and reduce the heat to low.
6. Simmer the mixture until the steak is fork tender, which takes about 1 1/2 to 2 hours.
7. Serve with mashed potatoes.
